findings.title: Daily-salt for IP/UA hashing is public-derivable
findings.description: hashIp and hashUserAgent use YYYY-MM-DD as the salt. SHA-256 over IPv4 keyspace (2^32) brute-forces in ~5 min on commodity GPUs. Daily salt is public so doesn't slow this down.
findings.exploit_scenario: Attacker gains read access to consent_audit.ip_hash (DB compromise / RLS misconfig / subpoena), brute-forces IPv4 keyspace per row using the row's date as salt, reverses ip_hash to IP, cross-references geo-IP/ISP records, re-identifies users to political opinions.
findings.impact: Defeats stated PII-minimization. Re-identification of users to political-views consent records, classified as sensitive under CO/CT/VA/UT privacy laws.
findings.recommendation: Move salt to a server-side secret (IP_HASH_SECRET env var, HMAC-SHA-256). Or truncate hash to 8-12 hex chars + store coarse geo separately. Never use a deterministic public-derivable salt.
findings.playbook: 1. Add IP_HASH_SECRET to env (32+ random bytes). 2. Change geo.ts:hashIp/hashUserAgent to HMAC-SHA-256(secret, ip). 3. Document rotation cadence (monthly). 4. Verify secret never appears in logs or response bodies.

findings.title: /api/match has no rate limiting; LLM cost amplification attack
findings.description: TODO comment acknowledges plan-specified rate limits (10/hr session, 30/hr IP) are not implemented. In-memory cache keyed on (race_id, free_text) so unique suffixes bypass it. MATCH_API_DISABLED is a reactive kill switch, not preventive.
findings.exploit_scenario: Attacker scripts POST /api/match with random suffix per request. Cache misses every time. Each call triggers Haiku (~$0.001). 60 req/min for 1 hr = ~$3.60/hr per IP. Distributed across 10 IPs = ~$864/day.
findings.impact: Financial — direct Anthropic spend amplification once API key is set in production. Plan §6 called out a $50/day spend cap as a required safeguard; not implemented.
findings.recommendation: Before enabling Anthropic in prod, ship at least one of: (a) Vercel KV / Upstash Redis token-bucket middleware keyed on session+IP, (b) persist llm_matches to Supabase as planned for cross-session cache hits, (c) Anthropic dashboard hard daily spend cap as defense in depth.
findings.playbook: 1. Add @upstash/ratelimit + edge middleware. 2. Configure 10 req/hr per session_id, 30 req/hr per IP. 3. Return 429 with Retry-After header. 4. Set Anthropic dashboard daily limit at 2x expected usage as backstop.

findings.title: postcss <8.5.10 XSS via transitive Next 16 dep
findings.description: GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 (CVSS 6.1): postcss <8.5.10 has XSS via unescaped </style> in CSS stringify. Pulled in transitively via Next 16. App uses Tailwind 4 with author-only CSS — no user-controlled CSS path to postcss stringify.
findings.exploit_scenario: Theoretical only — requires user-controlled CSS reaching postcss stringify, which doesn't exist in this app's architecture.
findings.impact: Practical exploit surface ~0 in current state. Track for upstream patch.
findings.recommendation: Wait for Next 16.x patch. Re-audit when patch lands.
findings.playbook: Watch Next.js release notes for postcss bump. No action required immediately.
